# VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0002 - RFC 9000 Address-Validation Token Emission Verification

## Scope

This slice covers server-only address-validation token emission and the proof that repeated validation transitions do not create duplicate NEW_TOKEN traffic. It does not claim Retry-token generation or client-side token handling.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0005
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0011

## Verification Method

Focused unit tests plus runtime inspection around path-validation transitions and NEW_TOKEN frame formatting.

## Preconditions

- The Incursa.Quic solution builds successfully.
- The RFC 9000 token-emission requirement homes are discoverable by the test runner.
- The runtime keeps server-only NEW_TOKEN emission internal to the repository.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the two requirement-home tests for the token-emission slice.
- Confirm the helper payload round-trips through QuicFrameCodec.TryParseNewTokenFrame.
- Confirm repeated validation of the same address does not create a second NEW_TOKEN send effect.

## Expected Result

Server-role validation of a new address emits a protected NEW_TOKEN datagram exactly once per address, client-role validation does not emit NEW_TOKEN traffic, and the internal helper formats a valid NEW_TOKEN frame payload that round-trips through the codec.

## Evidence

- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0005.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P3-0011.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicFrameCodec.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicNewTokenFrame.cs

## Status

This verification artifact records the landed address-validation token-emission slice and points at the requirement-home and runtime evidence in the working tree.
